key_insight: A self-attention mesh surrogate can emulate PFEM free-surface fluid dynamics on evolving geometries while preserving mesh structure for stable long-horizon rollouts.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, scalable prediction of engineering free-surface flows, including non-Newtonian cases, with access to reconstructed fields like stress using finite-element operators.
method: Use mesh-preserving self-attention to learn node-to-node interactions on PFEM discretizations.
Compare standard attention with a linear attention variant for lower cost and better scalability.
Support remeshing/node redistribution during rollout to maintain mesh quality and stability.
evidence_signal: The models were tested on 2D and 3D free-surface benchmarks with changing geometry and material parameters, and they accurately predicted transient behavior and final configurations.
watch_item: Performance depends on high-quality PFEM data and robust remeshing/reconstruction pipelines; scaling to larger industrial cases may still expose stability or cost tradeoffs.
paper_title: Attention mechanism for scalable mesh-based neural surrogates of free-surface fluids
